description_long: This object is a medieval-style sword featuring a long, straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade appears metallic with a slightly weathered, reflective surface, suggesting it is made of steel or iron. The hilt consists of a crossguard with rounded ends, a cylindrical grip, and a prominent spherical pommel, all of which have a bronze or brass-like finish. The crossguard and pommel display a warm, golden-brown coloration, contrasting with the darker, metallic blade. The handle is simple and unwrapped, with no visible grip texture or ergonomic shaping. There are no visible engravings or decorative inlays on the blade or hilt, giving the sword a utilitarian appearance.
description: A medieval-style sword with a straight, double-edged steel blade and a bronze-colored crossguard and pommel. The design is simple and functional, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade and the rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the broad blade tapering to a point and the symmetrical crossguard.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's thickness and the cylindrical handle with spherical pommel.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the pommel, crossguard, and the blade's taper.
